question: Did Iran pose an imminent threat to the US?

sideA.label: White House
sideA.text: Iran was reviving nuclear weapons program, developing missiles that could reach the US.

sideB.label: Pentagon / Intel
sideB.text: Briefers told Congress Iran had no plans to strike US forces unless Israel attacked first. DNI: Iran "not building a nuclear weapon."

resolution: CONTESTED — Pentagon briefing directly undercuts the White House's central justification for strikes.

question: Was the USS Abraham Lincoln struck?

sideA.label: IRGC
sideA.text: Claimed 4 ballistic missiles hit the carrier.

sideB.label: CENTCOM
sideB.text: "The Lincoln was not hit. The missiles didn't even come close." Lincoln continued launching aircraft.

resolution: CENTCOM denial is authoritative — Lincoln confirmed operational with photo evidence.

question: How many died in the Minab school strike?

sideA.label: Iran (official)
sideA.text: Death toll revised upward from 5 to 148 (prosecutor) to 165–180 (governor, Health Ministry).

sideB.label: US / Israel
sideB.text: Israel says "not aware of any US or Israeli strike on a school." CNN geolocated video to school.

resolution: UNRESOLVED — School confirmed 200ft from IRGC base. Independent verification impossible under blackout.

question: How many died in the Jan 2026 protest crackdowns?

sideA.label: Iranian Government
sideA.text: 3,117 killed (official announcement January 21).

sideB.label: HRANA / TIME / ICHR
sideB.text: Estimates range from 7,000 confirmed names (HRANA) to 43,000 (ICHR). TIME cites ~30,000 in 48 hours.

resolution: HEAVILY CONTESTED — Internet blackout prevents verification. 10x+ gap between lowest and highest estimates.

question: Is this a "regime change" war?

sideA.label: Hegseth (DoD)
sideA.text: "This is not a so-called regime change war."

sideB.label: Actions taken
sideB.text: Khamenei assassinated. Trump: "take back your country." State TV hacked. Regime infrastructure systematically destroyed.

resolution: CONTRADICTORY — Official messaging denies regime change while actions clearly target regime collapse.

question: Was the Natanz nuclear facility struck by US-Israeli airstrikes?

sideA.label: Iranian Ambassador to IAEA
sideA.text: Accused the US and Israel of launching airstrikes on the Natanz uranium enrichment facility, condemning the attacks as unlawful.

sideB.label: IAEA Director-General
sideB.text: Stated the agency had no indication that any of Iran’s nuclear facilities, including Natanz, had been struck, noting the lack of elevated radiation levels.

resolution: CONTESTED — Conflicting reports from Iranian officials and the IAEA regarding the targeting of Natanz.

question: How many civilians have been killed in the US-Israeli bombing campaign on Iran?

sideA.label: HRANA
sideA.text: Reported over 1,000 civilian deaths, including at least 181 children under the age of ten, in the first five days of the bombing campaign.

sideB.label: US Department of Defense
sideB.text: Claims that military operations are targeting Iranian military infrastructure and efforts are made to minimize civilian casualties.

resolution: CONTESTED — Discrepancy between human rights organizations' reports and US military statements on civilian casualties.

question: Did a US submarine sink the Iranian frigate IRIS Dena?

sideA.label: Iranian Foreign Minister
sideA.text: Publicly acknowledged that a U.S. submarine sank the Iranian frigate near Sri Lanka, calling it an "atrocity" and warning of severe consequences.

sideB.label: US Navy
sideB.text: Has not confirmed or denied involvement in the sinking of the IRIS Dena, maintaining operational security.

resolution: CONTESTED — Iran's official acknowledgment contrasts with the US's lack of confirmation regarding the incident.

question: Is the US capable of intercepting all Iranian missile and drone attacks?

sideA.label: US Defense Secretary
sideA.text: Confirmed that while the US maintains air superiority over Iran, not all Iranian missile or drone attacks can be stopped.

sideB.label: Iranian Military
sideB.text: Claims that their missile and drone capabilities can penetrate US and Israeli air defenses, causing significant damage.

resolution: CONTESTED — US acknowledges limitations in intercepting all attacks, while Iran asserts the effectiveness of their offensive capabilities.

question: Are the US-Israeli attacks against Iran legal under international law?

sideA.label: UN Special Rapporteur
sideA.text: Stated that the joint US-Israeli strikes against Iran likely violate the UN Charter’s prohibition on aggression and lack any valid legal justification.

sideB.label: US State Department
sideB.text: Argues that the strikes are in self-defense against an imminent threat posed by Iran's nuclear and missile programs.

resolution: CONTESTED — Divergent interpretations of international law regarding the legality of the military actions.

question: Has Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to US-Israeli military actions?

sideA.label: Iranian IRGC
sideA.text: Issued warnings prohibiting vessel passage through the Strait, leading to an effective halt in shipping traffic.

sideB.label: US Navy
sideB.text: Reports indicate that while Iran has threatened closure, the Strait remains open with heightened military presence to ensure safe passage.

resolution: CONTESTED — Conflicting reports on the operational status of the Strait of Hormuz amid escalating tensions.

question: Did Iran launch missile strikes against Israel and U.S. military bases?

sideA.label: Iranian State Media
sideA.text: Confirmed missile strikes targeting Israeli cities and U.S. military bases in the region as retaliation for recent attacks.

sideB.label: U.S. Department of Defense
sideB.text: Acknowledged missile launches from Iran but stated that most were intercepted with minimal damage reported.

resolution: CONTESTED — Discrepancy between Iranian claims of successful strikes and U.S. reports of effective defense measures.

question: Did the U.S. coordinate with Israel in the recent military strikes on Iran?

sideA.label: U.S. Administration Officials
sideA.text: Confirmed joint military operations with Israel targeting Iranian facilities and leadership.

sideB.label: Iranian Government
sideB.text: Accused the U.S. of backing Israeli attacks but claimed to have 'solid proof' of U.S. involvement.

resolution: CONTESTED — While U.S. officials confirm coordination, Iran presents evidence alleging deeper U.S. involvement.

question: Did Hezbollah launch missile strikes against Israel from Lebanon?

sideA.label: Hezbollah
sideA.text: Confirmed launching multiple missiles from Lebanon into Israel in response to Israeli and U.S. strikes on Iran.

sideB.label: Israeli Defense Forces (IDF)
sideB.text: Reported intercepting one projectile and stated that others fell in open areas, causing minimal damage.

resolution: CONTESTED — Hezbollah claims successful strikes, while Israel reports minimal impact due to interceptions.

question: Did Iran conduct a cyberattack on Jordan's national wheat silos?

sideA.label: Jordanian National Cybersecurity Center
sideA.text: Confirmed thwarting a cyberattack targeting the electronic control systems of the country's national wheat silos, attributing the attack to Iran.

sideB.label: Iranian Government
sideB.text: Denied involvement in any cyberattacks against Jordan, calling the allegations baseless.

resolution: CONTESTED — Jordan attributes the cyberattack to Iran, while Iran denies any involvement.

question: Did Iran launch missile and drone attacks on the United Arab Emirates?

sideA.label: UAE Defense Ministry
sideA.text: Reported that Iran launched 189 ballistic missiles, 941 drone attacks, and 3 cruise missiles against the UAE, resulting in 3 deaths and 78 injuries.

sideB.label: Iranian Government
sideB.text: Acknowledged targeting military installations in the UAE but denied causing civilian casualties, stating efforts were made to avoid non-combatant areas.

resolution: CONTESTED — UAE reports civilian casualties from Iranian attacks, while Iran claims to have targeted only military sites.

question: Was Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei assassinated by U.S.-Israeli airstrikes?

sideA.label: Iranian Government
sideA.text: Confirmed the death of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei due to airstrikes conducted by the U.S. and Israel.

sideB.label: U.S. and Israeli Officials
sideB.text: Acknowledged conducting airstrikes targeting Iranian leadership but have not confirmed the death of Khamenei.

resolution: CONTESTED — Iran confirms Khamenei's death, while U.S. and Israeli officials have not provided confirmation.

question: Did Iran conduct cyber operations against U.S. and Israeli military systems?

sideA.label: Iranian State Media
sideA.text: Reported successful cyberattacks disrupting U.S. and Israeli military communications and infrastructure.

sideB.label: U.S. Department of Defense
sideB.text: Acknowledged attempts by Iranian cyber units but stated that all attacks were successfully defended against with no operational impact.

resolution: CONTESTED — Iran claims successful cyber operations, while the U.S. reports no significant impact from such attacks.

question: Was the deadly blast at Shajareh Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab caused by a U.S. airstrike?

sideA.label: AP News
sideA.text: Satellite imagery and expert analysis suggest the explosion was caused by a U.S. airstrike, with the blast killing over 165 people, mostly children.

sideB.label: U.S. Military
sideB.text: Neither the U.S. nor Israel accepted responsibility for the strike; however, the U.S. military acknowledged nearby operations and launched an internal investigation.

resolution: CONTESTED — Evidence points to U.S. involvement, but official confirmation is pending.

question: Did U.S.-Israeli airstrikes target the Natanz uranium enrichment facility?

sideA.label: Iranian Ambassador to IAEA
sideA.text: Accused the U.S. and Israel of launching airstrikes on the Natanz facility, condemning the attacks as unlawful.

sideB.label: IAEA Director-General
sideB.text: Stated the agency had no indication that any of Iran’s nuclear facilities, including Natanz, had been struck, noting the lack of elevated radiation levels.

resolution: CONTESTED — Conflicting reports from Iranian officials and the IAEA regarding the targeting of Natanz.

question: Has Iran's military been significantly debilitated by U.S.-Israeli strikes?

sideA.label: President Donald Trump
sideA.text: Declared that Iran's military forces have been 'knocked out,' with its navy, air force, radar systems, and air defense neutralized.

sideB.label: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi
sideB.text: Rejected the idea of a ceasefire and declared Iran's readiness to confront a U.S. ground invasion, indicating continued military capability.

resolution: CONTESTED — U.S. claims of debilitating Iran's military are contradicted by Iran's assertions of ongoing readiness.

question: Is the U.S.-Israeli military campaign in Iran aimed at regime change?

sideA.label: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth
sideA.text: Emphasized that the conflict aims to neutralize threats, not enact regime change, despite the killing of Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

sideB.label: Iranian Government
sideB.text: Accused the U.S. and Israel of targeting regime infrastructure and leadership, suggesting an intent toward regime change.

resolution: CONTESTED — Official U.S. statements deny regime change intent, but actions targeting Iranian leadership suggest otherwise.

question: Has Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz in response to U.S.-Israeli military actions?

sideA.label: Iranian IRGC
sideA.text: Issued warnings prohibiting vessel passage through the Strait, leading to an effective halt in shipping traffic.

sideB.label: U.S. Navy
sideB.text: Reports indicate that while Iran has threatened closure, the Strait remains open with heightened military presence to ensure safe passage.

resolution: CONTESTED — Conflicting reports on the operational status of the Strait of Hormuz amid escalating tensions.

question: Did Iran reach out to the CIA to discuss ending the conflict?

sideA.label: Jerusalem Post / Western intelligence sources
sideA.text: Iran reached out to the CIA through back channels to discuss a potential end to the conflict. Trump reportedly said 'it's too late' for such discussions.

sideB.label: Iranian Foreign Ministry
sideB.text: Iran's Foreign Ministry says there is no room to discuss a ceasefire while military attacks continue. Foreign Minister Araghchi: 'We are not asking for a ceasefire. We don't see any reason why we should negotiate.'

resolution: CONTESTED — Reports of a backchannel conflict with public statements from both sides rejecting negotiations. Possible disconnect between Iranian diplomatic and military leadership.

question: Did Iran stop attacking Gulf neighbors after Pezeshkian's apology?

sideA.label: Iranian President Pezeshkian
sideA.text: Announced on March 7 that Iran would stop striking neighbors unless an attack originated from their territory. Issued a rare apology to neighboring countries for retaliatory attacks.

sideB.label: Gulf state defense ministries
sideB.text: Iranian drone and missile attacks on Gulf countries continued after Pezeshkian's announcement, including strikes on Dubai, Bahrain (Bapco refinery), Fujairah, Qatar (17 missiles), and Saudi Arabia (Al-Kharj) on March 9.

resolution: CONTRADICTORY — Pezeshkian's public apology and promise to stop Gulf attacks was immediately undercut by continued IRGC strikes. Internal power struggle between president and IRGC apparent.

question: How much is the US war on Iran costing per day?

sideA.label: CNN / Center for American Progress / CSIS
sideA.text: CNN analysts estimate $890M+ per day. Center for American Progress estimated $5B by Day 4. CSIS placed the first 100-hour cost at $3.7B. Total projected to exceed $10B by Day 11.

sideB.label: Trump Administration
sideB.text: Trump characterized the energy price spike as 'a small price to pay' to eliminate Iran's nuclear threat. Administration has not released official cost figures.

resolution: EVOLVING — Independent estimates vary widely but all point to significant daily expenditure. Official figures not released.

question: Has the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz triggered a global energy crisis?

sideA.label: Rystad Energy / Stimson Center / RBC Capital Markets
sideA.text: Traffic through the strait has fallen to near zero. 20 million barrels/day of oil — roughly 20% of global supply — is blocked. Analysts warn of potential $200/bbl oil and a global recession if the closure persists. 'Biggest energy crisis since the 1970s embargo.' European gas prices nearly doubled after Iran struck Qatari LNG facilities.

sideB.label: US Navy / Trump Administration
sideB.text: US Navy maintains a heightened military presence and insists the strait remains technically open. Trump has threatened to hit Iran '20 times harder' if it stops tankers. Strategic Petroleum Reserve release is under consideration.

resolution: EFFECTIVELY CLOSED — Commercial traffic has halted due to IRGC drone threats; US Navy presence has not restored civilian passage. Iran reportedly allowing only Chinese-flagged vessels to transit.

question: Did Iran use internationally banned cluster munitions against Israeli civilians?

sideA.label: Al Jazeera / Israeli authorities
sideA.text: An Iranian missile with a cluster warhead struck residential areas of Yehud on March 10, killing 2 civilians and wounding dozens across multiple strike sites.

sideB.label: Iranian Military
sideB.text: Iran has not commented specifically on the Yehud strike. IRGC maintains its missiles target military infrastructure. Iran is not a signatory to the Convention on Cluster Munitions.

resolution: CONFIRMED — Multiple reports confirm cluster warhead used; civilian deaths verified. Raises international humanitarian law concerns. Neither Israel nor US are CCM signatories either.

question: Is Mojtaba Khamenei's first 'statement' as Supreme Leader actually from him?

sideA.label: Iranian State Media
sideA.text: Broadcast a statement attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei on March 12 calling for Hormuz to remain closed, US bases to close, and vowing revenge. A photo of Khamenei was shown while another person read the remarks.

sideB.label: CNN / Iran International / Al Jazeera analysts
sideB.text: Khamenei did not appear in person. It is unclear whether the statement actually came from him. His health and exact whereabouts remain unknown. Iran analyst Arash Azizi told CNN the statement gives 'very little hope to Iranians.'

resolution: CONTESTED — Statement attributed to new Supreme Leader but not delivered directly by him. Authenticity and condition of Mojtaba Khamenei unverified.

question: Has the Pentagon confirmed US responsibility for the Iranian girls' school strike?

sideA.label: Pentagon (preliminary assessment, March 12)
sideA.text: Pentagon opened a formal investigation after a preliminary internal assessment suggested the US was at fault for the missile strike on the Shajareh Tayyebeh Elementary School in Minab, which killed at least 165 civilians, many children.

sideB.label: US Military (earlier statements)
sideB.text: Earlier, neither the US nor Israel accepted responsibility. US military said it launched an internal investigation and the school was near an IRGC Navy base.

resolution: INVESTIGATION ONGOING — Preliminary Pentagon assessment indicates US fault; formal investigation opened March 12. No final determination.

question: Is Iran unified on peace terms, or is there a split between president and supreme leader?

sideA.label: President Pezeshkian (March 12)
sideA.text: Outlined three conditions to end the war: recognition of legitimate rights, payment of reparations, and firm international guarantees against future aggression.

sideB.label: Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei (March 12)
sideB.text: Statement offered no off-ramp, no conditions, no ceasefire signal. Called for Hormuz to remain closed, all US bases to close, and vowed revenge remains 'an open file.' Also threatened expansion of war to new fronts.

resolution: CONTRADICTORY — Pezeshkian's conditional peace terms directly contradict Khamenei's defiant, no-ceasefire stance. Analysts see an internal power struggle between Iran's political and religious-military leadership.

question: Will the IEA's record 400M barrel reserve release stabilize oil markets?

sideA.label: IEA Executive Director Fatih Birol / G7 Finance Ministers
sideA.text: IEA agreed to release 400M barrels — the largest in history — with US contributing 172M from SPR. Goal is to offset supply disruption. G7 also ready to release energy stockpiles. IEA: 'Largest supply disruption in the history of the global oil market.'

sideB.label: Analysts / Iranian Military
sideB.text: Analysts warn stockpile releases can only partially offset prolonged Gulf disruption. Despite the announcement, Brent spiked past $100/bbl after Khamenei vowed Hormuz stays closed. Iran's military warned 'Get ready for oil at $200 a barrel.'

resolution: CONTESTED — Reserve release announced but oil still above $100/bbl. Insufficient to fully offset 20M bbl/day normally transiting Hormuz if blockade continues.

question: Was the US KC-135 tanker crash in Iraq due to hostile fire?

sideA.label: US CENTCOM
sideA.text: The incident that downed a KC-135 Stratotanker occurred in 'friendly airspace' and was 'not due to hostile or friendly fire.' A rescue operation was launched for the 6 crew members onboard.

sideB.label: Islamic Resistance in Iraq
sideB.text: Claimed responsibility for shooting down the US KC-135 aircraft with 'the appropriate weapon' over western Iraq during Operation Epic Fury.

resolution: CONTESTED — CENTCOM denies hostile fire while Iran-backed Iraqi militias claim credit. Cause of crash unverified independently; investigation ongoing. No ejection seats on KC-135 compounds crew survival concerns.

question: Is the US strike on Kharg Island a legitimate military target or an attack on civilian infrastructure?

sideA.label: Pentagon / US Administration
sideA.text: Kharg Island oil infrastructure funds Iran's war machine and is a legitimate military-economic target. The US warned oil facilities would be targeted if Iran did not end the Hormuz blockade. 'Ramping up' the campaign is necessary to end the conflict faster.

sideB.label: Iran / UN / International Community
sideB.text: Kharg Island is civilian energy infrastructure. Striking it constitutes economic warfare targeting Iran's entire population. Iran warns the strike will push oil to $200/bbl and devastate global energy markets. UN Charter prohibits deliberate destruction of civilian economic infrastructure.

resolution: CONTESTED — Pentagon frames as military-economic target; international law experts and Iran condemn it as unlawful attack on civilian infrastructure. Kharg handles 90%+ of Iran's crude exports.

question: Did Iran's attack on tankers near Basra trigger a halt to Iraqi oil exports?

sideA.label: Bloomberg / Iraq state oil marketer (SOMO)
sideA.text: Iraq's state oil marketer confirmed southern oil export terminals suspended operations following Iranian underwater drone attacks on tankers Safesea Vishnu and Zefyros in Iraqi territorial waters near Basra.

sideB.label: Iranian Government
sideB.text: Iran confirmed the operation, stating underwater drones 'blew up two oil tankers in the Persian Gulf.' Iran claimed vessels were US-owned and legitimate military targets, making no reference to Iraqi civilian export infrastructure.

resolution: CONFIRMED — Iraqi oil terminal suspension verified. First oil-related attack in Iraqi waters during the conflict. Iraq exports ~3.3M bbl/day; disruption compounds global supply crisis.

question: Is Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei alive?

sideA.label: Trump / Hegseth / US State Dept
sideA.text: Trump: 'I'm hearing he's not alive — if he is, he should surrender.' Hegseth said Khamenei is 'likely disfigured.' State Dept offered $10M reward for information on Khamenei's whereabouts and condition. US intelligence assesses status as 'unknown but degraded.'

sideB.label: Iranian State Media
sideB.text: A statement attributed to Mojtaba Khamenei was broadcast March 12, but he did not appear in person — a photo was shown while another person read the statement. Iranian state media insists the Supreme Leader is 'leading' Iran's defense.

resolution: UNVERIFIED — No confirmed sighting of Mojtaba Khamenei since being named Supreme Leader on March 8. Statement attribution contested. US and Israeli intelligence publicly doubt his condition.

question: Is Iran secretly seeking negotiations while publicly rejecting ceasefire?

sideA.label: Trump / NBC News
sideA.text: Trump told reporters on March 15 that Iran is 'ready to make a deal' but that terms are 'not good enough yet.' NBC News reported Iran sent back-channel signals through third parties expressing interest in talks.

sideB.label: Araghchi / Iranian Foreign Ministry
sideB.text: FM Araghchi on CBS Face the Nation (March 15): 'We never asked for a ceasefire, and we have never asked even for negotiation.' Iranian Foreign Ministry said publicly there is 'nothing to talk about' while bombing continues.

resolution: CONTRADICTORY — Possible disconnect between civilian-diplomatic and military-IRGC tracks within Iran. Trump may be projecting willingness onto Iranian back-channels not confirmed by Iranian principals.

question: Will other nations send warships to join a US-led Hormuz escort coalition?

sideA.label: Trump / White House
sideA.text: Trump publicly urged other nations to send warships to keep the Strait of Hormuz open, stating 'the countries that get the oil' should protect the passage. Ambassador Waltz called for a multinational naval coalition similar to CTF-151.

sideB.label: Allied governments / analysts
sideB.text: No nation has publicly committed forces to a Hormuz escort coalition as of March 16. EU extended Operation Aspides mandate but has not sent ships into the Strait under Iranian fire. Gulf states have not offered naval assistance. Analysts note the risk of losing ships to IRGC anti-ship missiles is deterring participation.

resolution: UNRESOLVED — US coalition call has received no public commitments. Meaningful naval coalition formation under fire remains uncertain as of Day 17.
